name: Salem Academy
about: Salem Academy is an independent college-preparatory boarding and day school for girls in Winston-Salem, North Carolina, founded in 1772 by the Moravian Church. It is the oldest girls' school in continuous operation in the United States and shares a campus with Salem College, allowing students to take college-level coursework. The school reports 100 percent college acceptance for its graduates.
college_matriculation: 100% of graduates continue to college; students may earn college credit through Salem College coursework
accreditation: Southern Association of Colleges and Schools (SACS); member NAIS
notable_alumni: Sarah Childress Polk, Sarah T. Hughes, Tillie Kidd Fowler, Vivian Howard, Marshall Chapman, Elizabeth Campbell
financial_aid_notes: Uses an Indexed Tuition model customized to each family's ability to pay; merit-based scholarships available; NC residents may qualify for NCSEAA Opportunity Scholarships ($3,000-$7,000/year). Uses SSS/TADS.

name: Andrews Osborne Academy
about: Andrews Osborne Academy is an independent, coeducational boarding and day school in Willoughby, Ohio, about 20 miles east of Cleveland, founded in 1910. It enrolls roughly 350 students from PreK through grade 12 plus postgraduate, with students from several states and around 20 countries. The school offers the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme and the Cambridge AICE program in addition to AP courses.
college_matriculation: 100% of graduates accepted to a U.S. college or university
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS)
financial_aid_notes: About 36% of students (grades 1-11) receive need-based aid with an average award of $11,200; also EdChoice Scholarship, Cleveland Scholarship, and merit scholarships. Uses Clarity Application.

name: Gilmour Academy
about: Gilmour Academy is an independent, Roman Catholic, coeducational college-preparatory day and boarding school in Gates Mills, Ohio, founded in 1946 by the Brothers of Holy Cross. It serves students from toddler through grade 12 plus postgraduates on a 144-acre campus, with a boarding program for grades 7-12. Resident students make up about 16 percent of the Upper School and represent multiple states and countries.
college_matriculation: About 20.6% to top-50 U.S. universities; notable matriculations across selective colleges
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS); member NAIS, OAIS
notable_alumni: Gary Cohn, Art Rooney II, John W. Snow, Phoebe Robinson, Paul Dolan, Matthew J. Dolan
financial_aid_notes: Need-based awards available for grades K-12, varying with demonstrated need; Glen Oak Scholarship Fund among resources.

name: Grand River Academy
about: Grand River Academy is an independent, nonsectarian, college-preparatory boarding and day school for boys in Austinburg, Ohio, founded in 1831 by Dr. Orestes K. Hawley. It serves grades 8-12 plus a postgraduate option on a roughly 200-acre rural campus in Ashtabula County. The school maintains a low student-teacher ratio and small enrollment of around 98 students.
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS); member NAIS, TABS
notable_alumni: Includes the founder of B.F. Goodrich, U.S. Representatives and Senators, and Major League Baseball figures
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial aid offered to all families through School & Student Services (SSS); Ohio EdChoice Scholarship available for eligible students.

name: Maumee Valley Country Day School
about: Maumee Valley Country Day School is an independent, coeducational, non-religious PreK-12 school in Toledo, Ohio, founded in 1884 (with roots tracing to 1842). It is the only PreK-12 accredited independent school in Northwest Ohio and operates an international boarding program (Dayal House) for grades 9-12. Around 20 percent of students are international, and the average ACT score is reported at 28.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend Brown, Carleton, Case Western Reserve, Colby, Columbia, Swarthmore, UC Berkeley, University of Michigan
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS); member NAIS, OAIS
notable_alumni: Thomas L. Ashley, Sean B. Carroll, Carty Finkbeiner, Phil Griffin, Michelle Rhee, Alyson Stoner
financial_aid_notes: Offers merit scholarships, need-based financial aid and grants, and payment plans.

name: Olney Friends School
about: Olney Friends School is a small coeducational, progressive Quaker college-preparatory boarding and day school in Barnesville, Ohio, founded in 1837 and affiliated with the Religious Society of Friends. It serves grades 9-12 on a 350-acre rural campus that includes a certified organic farm in the Appalachian foothills. The school enrolls roughly 35-40 students, the large majority of whom board, and acceptance to a four-year college is a graduation requirement.
college_matriculation: Acceptance into a four-year college is a graduation requirement
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS); member NAIS, OAIS, Friends Council on Education, TABS
notable_alumni: Louis J. Taber, Chris Dickerson, Patricia Altschul, Shira Tarrant
financial_aid_notes: Offers need-based tuition assistance; about 95% of students receive financial aid. Uses FACTS for financial aid applications.
